natural_language: There are six labeled blocks on the table. Most of them form a tall stack: from the surface going up, A is at the bottom, B sits on top of A, then C on B, and D crowns the top of this four-block pillar. Off to the sides, E and F are each sitting directly on the table by themselves, nothing above or below them except the table surface. The only block you can grab right now without unstacking is D (the top of the pillar), plus the two solo blocks E and F. The arm mechanism is empty-handed. Your objective: flip the four-block pillar upside-down so that A ends up on B, B on C, C on D, and D touches the table. The two loitering blocks E and F must remain on the table, completely undisturbed. Clear?
